data.title: Enel Chile Approves US$216 Million Final Dividend from 2025 Earnings
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Enel Chile SA ( (ENIC) ).

At its ordinary shareholders’ meeting held on April 28, 2026, Enel Chile S.A. approved a final dividend for fiscal year 2025 of US$268.8 million, equivalent to 50% of its 2025 net income, from which the interim dividend paid in January 2026 will be deducted. After this deduction, shareholders will receive a remaining payout of US$216.0 million, or an exact US$0.003123519916077 per share, underscoring the company’s adherence to a sizable cash-return policy.

The dividend, charged against 2025 profits, will be paid in Chilean pesos on May 20, 2026, using the official U.S. dollar exchange rate published on May 13, 2026. The decision signals continued cash generation and a commitment to shareholder remuneration in Chile’s regulated utilities sector, with operational details for collection and deposit managed via DCV Registros S.A. and standard local market procedures.

The most recent analyst rating on (ENIC) stock is a Hold with a $4.60 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Enel Chile SA stock, see the ENIC Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on ENIC Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ENIC is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by mid-range financial quality (stronger 2025 margins and reasonable leverage but multi-year volatility and uneven cash conversion) and an attractive valuation (low P/E and high dividend yield). These positives are partially offset by weak technical momentum, with the stock trading below key moving averages and negative MACD.

To see Spark’s full report on ENIC stock, click here.

More about Enel Chile SA

Enel Chile S.A. is a publicly traded power and utilities company based in Santiago, operating in Chile’s electricity sector. The company is part of the Enel group and focuses on generation, distribution, and commercialization of electric power for residential, commercial, and industrial customers in the Chilean market.

Average Trading Volume: 688,501

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $6.26B

data.title: Gogoro Shareholders Approve Major Increase in Authorized Capital at 2026 AGM
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Gogoro ( (GGR) ) just unveiled an update.

Gogoro Inc., a Taiwan-based technology company and global leader in battery-swapping ecosystems for electric mobility, serves nearly 700,000 riders who have completed more than 800 million battery swaps across over 2,700 GoStation locations. The company’s focus on sustainable, smart urban transportation has earned it multiple global awards, highlighting its growing impact in climate-tech and city mobility infrastructure.

On April 28, 2026, Gogoro held its Annual General Meeting of Shareholders, and on April 29, 2026 it announced that all resolutions put to shareholders were approved. Investors backed a significant increase in authorized share capital, adding 37,500,000 authorized but unissued ordinary shares, approved a mechanism to adjourn the AGM if more proxies are needed in future, and authorized directors and officers to take all necessary steps to implement these decisions, moves that expand Gogoro’s financing flexibility and operational latitude for future corporate actions.

The capital expansion provides Gogoro with a larger pool of shares it can issue for potential fundraising, strategic investments or other corporate purposes, potentially strengthening its ability to compete and scale in the fast-evolving electric mobility market. The shareholder approvals also streamline corporate governance by empowering management to execute required filings and registry updates, aligning the company’s legal structure with its growth ambitions.

Spark’s Take on GGR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GGR is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial fundamentals—ongoing net losses, negative free cash flow, and reduced balance-sheet flexibility versus debt—despite clear 2025 operating improvements. The earnings call supports a credible profitability roadmap and near-term funding, while technicals are bullish but overbought, and valuation signals remain constrained by losses and no dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on GGR stock, click here.

More about Gogoro

Gogoro Inc. is a Taiwan-based global technology company specializing in battery-swapping ecosystems for electric two-wheelers and broader sustainable urban mobility. Founded in 2011, it operates the Gogoro Network, which supports nearly 700,000 riders with over 800 million battery swaps completed across more than 2,700 GoStation locations, positioning the firm as a leading player in smart, low-emission transportation infrastructure.

The company has garnered international recognition for its climate-tech and innovation leadership, with 2024 accolades from Fortune, Fast Company, MIT Technology Review and Frost & Sullivan underscoring its role in advancing battery-swapping as a viable alternative to conventional fueling. This global profile bolsters Gogoro’s influence as cities seek scalable solutions to reduce pollution and transition to cleaner mobility systems.

Average Trading Volume: 12,865

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $61.31M

data.title: Ryvyl Nears Final Court Approval of SEC Settlement
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ryvyl ( (RVYL) ) has provided an update.

In July 2025, Ryvyl Inc. reached a settlement with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission that imposed no monetary penalty and included no admission of wrongdoing by the company. The SEC has now approved the terms of that settlement and filed a complaint and related materials in federal court in California to formally memorialize the resolution, with the company emphasizing that the filing introduces no new or unresolved claims and that the matter will be fully closed once the district court endorses the agreed judgment, which is expected shortly.

Under the settlement, Ryvyl has consented to a final judgment that permanently restrains and enjoins it from violating key antifraud and reporting provisions of the federal securities laws, while waiving its rights to a jury trial, appeal, and certain fee claims. The company also acknowledges potential collateral regulatory consequences from the permanent injunction, including possible statutory disqualifications with self-regulatory organizations, underscoring that while the SEC civil case is resolved, the compliance and regulatory implications will remain relevant for its operations and stakeholders.

Spark’s Take on RVYL Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, RVYL is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by deteriorating financial performance (sharp 2025 revenue contraction, persistent losses, and a major swing to negative operating/free cash flow). Technicals are mixed with modest near-term stabilization but a longer-term downtrend, while valuation remains challenged due to unprofitability. Corporate events provide some offset via merger progress and Nasdaq compliance, but do not fully counter the underlying financial risk.

To see Spark’s full report on RVYL stock, click here.

More about Ryvyl

Ryvyl Inc. operates in the financial technology sector, focusing on digital payments and related services for businesses and consumers. The company’s platform and products are positioned within the regulated U.S. securities environment, making its compliance posture a key factor for investors and market partners.

Average Trading Volume: 46,301

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: $7.17M

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Mission Produce ( (AVO) ) has provided an update.

At a special meeting of stockholders held on April 28, 2026, Mission Produce shareholders approved the issuance of new common shares required to complete its planned mergers with Calavo Growers under a previously announced merger agreement. Roughly 70% of outstanding shares were represented, and the share issuance proposal passed comfortably, satisfying a key Nasdaq-related approval for the transaction.

An adjournment proposal intended to allow more time to solicit proxies, if needed, was rendered unnecessary because sufficient votes were already secured to approve the share issuance. The vote clears a major procedural hurdle toward closing the mergers, which are expected to be completed in the fiscal quarter ending July 31, 2026, subject to customary conditions and transaction risks highlighted by the companies, including potential delays, costs, and the possibility that anticipated synergies may not be realized.

The most recent analyst rating on (AVO) stock is a Buy with a $18.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Mission Produce stock, see the AVO Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on AVO Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, AVO is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by strengthened financial footing (very low leverage, restored profitability, positive free cash flow) but tempered by near-term top-line/pricing headwinds and guidance for weaker Q2 profitability. Technicals are supportive but not strong, while valuation metrics (negative P/E and no dividend yield provided) reduce the overall score.

To see Spark’s full report on AVO stock, click here.

More about Mission Produce

Mission Produce, Inc. is a U.S.-based company in the fresh produce industry, focused primarily on avocados and related produce. The company operates across the avocado supply chain, from sourcing to distribution, and targets both domestic and international retail, wholesale, and foodservice markets.

Calavo Growers, Inc. operates in the same sector, with a core business in avocados and other fresh food products, and the planned combination with Mission Produce reflects ongoing consolidation in the produce industry as companies seek greater scale and efficiency.

data.title: CN Healthy Food Tech Faces Offshore Listing Penalties
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

CN Healthy Food Tech Group ( (UCFI) ) has shared an announcement.

On April 24, 2026, CN Healthy Food Tech Group’s subsidiaries Zhong Guo Liang Tou and Zhongneng Liangke received an advance notice of administrative penalty from the Heilongjiang arm of the China Securities Regulatory Commission, which concluded an investigation into the company’s September 2025 merger with Iron Horse Acquisition Company and Nasdaq listing. Regulators found the company failed to complete mandatory offshore listing filing procedures under China’s Trial Administrative Measures, and indicated plans to impose fines totaling RMB3,000,000 on Zhongneng Liangke and RMB1,500,000 on CEO and chairman Zhenjun Jiang, who now have a brief window to respond, highlighting increasing regulatory scrutiny of Chinese companies’ overseas listings and potential financial and governance repercussions for the group.

More about CN Healthy Food Tech Group

CN Healthy Food Tech Group Corp., through its subsidiaries Zhong Guo Liang Tou Group Limited and Heilongjiang Zhongneng Liangke Agricultural Technology, operates in China’s agricultural technology and healthy food sector. The group is focused on leveraging agri-tech capabilities, with its securities listed on the Nasdaq capital market following a merger completed in September 2025.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $287.8M

Learn more about UCFI stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: YHI International Reshapes Board With New Executive Director Appointment
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from YHI International Limited ( (SG:BPF) ) is now available.

YHI International Limited has reshaped its leadership by appointing Ryan Tay Guoren as an executive director with effect from 29 April 2026, while long-serving executive director Tay Tiang Guan stepped down at the close of the company’s annual general meeting on the same day. Following these changes, the board will comprise Executive Chairman and Group Managing Director Tay Tian Hoe Richard, new Executive Director Ryan Tay and three independent non-executive directors, with the compositions of the audit, nominating and remuneration committees remaining unchanged to preserve continuity in governance and oversight structures.

The company’s decision to refresh its executive ranks while leaving its key board committees intact signals a calibrated transition that blends new management input with stability in risk, nomination and pay oversight. This balance may reassure investors and other stakeholders that leadership succession is being managed without disrupting the core governance framework that underpins the group’s listed-company obligations and strategic direction.

More about YHI International Limited

YHI International Limited is a Singapore-based group whose board structure suggests it operates as a diversified corporate entity with an executive chairman, executive director and a slate of independent non-executive directors. The company maintains standard governance practices through dedicated audit, nominating and remuneration committees, indicating a focus on regulatory compliance and oversight in its listed environment.

Average Trading Volume: 41,540

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: S$113.3M

An announcement from China Overseas Property Holdings ( (HK:2669) ) is now available.

China Overseas Property Holdings has renewed a series of continuing connected transactions with fellow group members China Overseas Land & Investment and China State Construction, covering property leasing arrangements from July 1, 2026 to June 30, 2029. The new leasing agreements allow the group to continue renting properties from these related parties under specified annual caps, supporting its operational footprint across managed projects.

The company has also renewed and expanded its property management utilities charges arrangements with China Overseas Land & Investment for the same three-year period, adding responsibility for utilities in public areas of commercial properties alongside individual units. As these deals are with connected parties under the same ultimate parent, they fall under Hong Kong connected transaction rules, underscoring the group’s ongoing reliance on intra-group property and service arrangements for its business model.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2669) stock is a Sell with a HK$5.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Overseas Property Holdings stock, see the HK:2669 Stock Forecast page.

The company focuses on managing residential and commercial properties, including leasing arrangements and associated utilities services, primarily within developments linked to its connected group companies.

data.title: Anhui Conch Cement Posts Lower Q1 2026 Profit and Revenue Amid Weaker Operating Cash Flow
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Anhui Conch Cement Company ( (HK:0914) ) just unveiled an update.

Anhui Conch Cement reported its unaudited first-quarter 2026 results, prepared under PRC GAAP and covering the period from 1 January to 31 March 2026. The company confirmed management’s responsibility for the truthfulness and completeness of the figures, noting that its quarterly report will also be published in PRC newspapers on 30 April 2026.

Revenue for the quarter fell 10.45% year-on-year to RMB 17.07 billion, while net profit attributable to equity shareholders dropped 18.98% to RMB 1.47 billion. Operating cash flow decreased 35.28%, and the weighted average return on net assets slipped by 0.20 percentage point, although total assets and shareholders’ equity were broadly stable, with slight adjustments reflecting the January 2026 common-control acquisition of Anhui Conch Green Energy Power Sales Co., Ltd.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0914) stock is a Buy with a HK$31.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Anhui Conch Cement Company stock, see the HK:0914 Stock Forecast page.

More about Anhui Conch Cement Company

Anhui Conch Cement Company Limited is a major Chinese cement producer operating in the building materials industry, with its shares listed in Hong Kong and Shanghai. The group manufactures and sells cement and related products, serving construction and infrastructure markets across mainland China and potentially other regions where it distributes its output.

Endur ASA ( (DE:4ZT0) ) has issued an announcement.

Engelsen Total, which is set to become a subsidiary of Total Betong and ultimately Endúr ASA, has secured a turnkey contract worth about NOK 60 million from Hydro Husnes in Kvinnherad, Norway. The project covers engineering and construction of changing room and industrial safety facilities, including groundworks and infrastructure connections, with construction scheduled to start in the third quarter of 2026 and an expected 12‑month build period, reinforcing Endúr’s role in critical industrial infrastructure delivery.

More about Endur ASA

Endúr ASA is a leading Scandinavian contractor headquartered in Lysaker and listed on the Oslo Stock Exchange. The company focuses on critical infrastructure such as bridges, quays, tunnels and dams, operating a decentralized model that lets subsidiaries retain technical expertise and strong local positions while pursuing strategic acquisitions in fragmented niche markets.
